lenses.guidance: vv 6-18's repeated 'do not have sexual relations with...' (Hebrew lo tegalleh ervah, 'do not uncover the nakedness of') forms a litany of imperatives. The Hebrew formula governs the chapter — relations named by negation.

v 26's command for the native and the sojourner alike echoes the equality logic running across Scripture from Lev 19:34 to Eph 2:11-22. Holiness applies inside Israel and to those who join her — a canon-wide thread.

vv 24-28's land-vomits-out motif (the same image at v 25, v 28) reframes sexual sin as covenant pollution. The promise of the land is contingent on Israel's holiness — exile threatens the redemptive arc itself.
